","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Use the fixed-step budget more effectively by aligning cosine decay to TRAIN_MAX_STEPS, switching to AdamW, and modestly increasing model capacity with a wider effective channel count and an extra residual block.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The current model is still underfitting within the fixed step budget, so a slightly more aggressive AdamW step size with a bit less decay is the simplest next knob to improve convergence speed.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.2,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )
","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The model is already large enough to benefit from a more aggressive short-horizon schedule, and OneCycle is a better fit than the current custom warmup/cosine setup for squeezing extra gain out of 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The OneCycle schedule is already doing the heavy lifting; this is a small step down in peak learning rate to let the wider model settle a bit better in the last part of the short run.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last reduction in peak LR hurt, so the next clean test is a slightly higher OneCycle peak to see if the larger model still benefits from more aggressive early updates.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider convolutional trunk is already in place; the remaining cheap capacity lever is the classifier head, and restoring the better 2e-3 peak LR should keep the short OneCycle run from becoming too aggressive.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger head did not help, so restore the tighter classifier and back off weight decay a bit to reduce regularization pressure on the still-underfitting run.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.2,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=1000.0,
    )
","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Restore the decay setting that was working better and let the OneCycle schedule finish with a much lower tail LR so the model can settle more cleanly at the end of the short run.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(

    )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore the better OneCycle tail from the best run and add a small amount of label smoothing to reduce overconfident fits without changing the model or step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing has been the only recent regularizer that helped, so a slightly stronger value is a low-cost way to test whether the model still wants a bit more calibration pressure.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.08)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current run is near a local optimum, so a small reduction in label smoothing is the safest remaining regularization tweak to test whether the model benefits from slightly sharper targets.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.08)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The weaker smoothing underperformed, so restore the better regularization point that was giving the lowest loss so far.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is already close to the optimum, so a slightly stronger AdamW decay is a reasonable last-mile regularization test alongside the label smoothing that has been helping.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last change increased regularization and hurt, so revert to the better decay level that matched the best visible run while keeping the current scheduler and model intact.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.09)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The best setting is already clustered near 0.1, so a small step down is the cleanest remaining test for whether the optimum is just below that point.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.09)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The 0.09 smoothing step regressed, and 0.1 has already produced the best visible loss, so restoring it is the safest remaining move.","primary_mode":"caching"}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.25,","old":"        pct_start=0.2,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The best run is already close to the current setting, so a slightly longer warmup is the most plausible remaining scheduler nudge for the wider model.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        pct_start=0.2,","old":"        pct_start=0.25,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The longer warmup regressed, and the previously best run used the 0.2 start fraction, so restoring that setting is the safest final scheduler choice.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
